sectionNote: Spec pieces, cut like client work. Every edit below was built against a real business objective — awareness, retention, authority — to show exactly what I'd do with your footage. No invented numbers, no borrowed logos.

items.description: Spec edit — a brand-film and reel system for a neighborhood cafe. Scripted and cut to show how I'd make a local spot the place people have already seen online before they walk in. No client brief behind it: this is the pitch.
Spec edit — long-form structure built to hold attention past the ten-minute mark. Story arc, pacing, and packaging, cut on spec to show what I'd do with a real client's raw footage.
Concept edit — a talking-head system with pattern interrupts, captions, and retention-first pacing. Built to show founders exactly how their episodes would be cut before a single one exists.

Concept edit — motion-graphics ad spots with kinetic type, clean transitions, and product-first storytelling, graded to sit next to broadcast work. Made on spec to show the launch treatment before there's a launch.
